name: La Seu d'Urgell and Calbinyà Loop Trail
name_original: La Seu de Urgell y Calbinyà — Sendero circular PR-C 62
description: Hike the moderate 5.8-mile PR-C 62 circular trail near La Seu d'Urgell, offering panoramic views and medieval heritage.
region_intro: Hiking around La Seu D'Urgell offers access to diverse landscapes within the Catalan Pyrenees. The region is characterized by Pyrenean foothills, the confluence of the Segre and Gran Valira rivers, and extensive forested mountains. It borders the Alt Pirineu Natural Park and the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, featuring high peaks, rocky gorges, and river valleys. The terrain includes meadows, springs, and varied elevations suitable for different hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around La Seu D'Urgell The most popular hiking route is La Seu d'Urgell and Calbinyà Loop Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) moderate trail that takes about 3 hours to complete, offering views of the surrounding Pyrenean landscape and the village of Calbinyà. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parc del Segre – Calle de los Canónigos loop from la Seu d'Urgell, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route explores the area around the Segre Olympic Park and the historic Calle de los Canónigos. Local hikers also love the Segalers Irrigation – Waterfall on the Canal Trail loop from la Seu d'Urgell, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through irrigation canals and a waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around La Seu D'Urgell is defined by Pyrenean foothills, river valleys, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in La Seu D'Urgell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 480 hikers have used komoot to explore La Seu D'Urgell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in La Seu D'Urgell?
A: La Seu D'Urgell offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 30 marked routes available. These trails cater to different skill levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging mountain ascents within the Pyrenean foothills and natural parks.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, La Seu D'Urgell has several easy hiking trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segalers Irrigation – Waterfall on the Canal Trail loop from la Seu d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.7-mile (5.9 km) route that takes about 1 hour 40 minutes. The region's total of 10 easy routes includes peaceful walks along the Segre River, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in La Seu D'Urgell?
A: Absolutely. The region offers numerous trails suitable for families, especially the easier routes that are up to 2 hours long and have minimal elevation gain. These paths often traverse meadows and riverbanks, providing a pleasant experience for all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segalers Irrigation – Waterfall on the Canal Trail loop&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of an easy, family-friendly option.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the trails around La Seu D'Urgell?
A: Hiking in La Seu D'Urgell offers diverse and stunning views. You can expect panoramic vistas of the Pyrenean foothills, the confluence of the Segre and Gran Valira rivers, and extensive forested mountains. Trails often provide glimpses into the Alt Pirineu and Cadí-Moixeró Natural Parks, showcasing high peaks, rocky gorges, and lush valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937136929/la-seu-d-urgell-and-calbinya-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Seu d'Urgell and Calbinyà Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers views of the surrounding Pyrenean landscape and the village of Calbinyà.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around La Seu D'Urgell are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14948894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc del Segre – Calle de los Canónigos loop from la Seu d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Segre Olympic Park and the historic Calle de los Canónigos. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20064620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calbinyà Village loop from la Seu d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in La Seu D'Urgell?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging mountain routes, and the stunning natural beauty of the Pyrenean landscape. The well-marked trails and the opportunity to explore both natural and cultural highlights are frequently mentioned.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead to natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segalers Irrigation – Waterfall on the Canal Trail loop from la Seu d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route specifically designed to take you through irrigation canals and past a waterfall, offering a refreshing natural highlight.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in La Seu D'Urgell?
A: La Seu D'Urgell offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for most trails. Summer is excellent for higher altitude routes within the nearby natural parks, though lower elevation trails can be warm. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower paths, but higher mountain routes may require alpine experience and specific gear due to snow and ice.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in La Seu D'Urgell?
A: While many trails in the region are generally accessible, it's important to check specific regulations for the Alt Pirineu Natural Park and Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, as rules regarding dogs can vary, especially concerning wildlife protection. Always keep your dog under control and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, hiking in La Seu D'Urgell can be combined with exploring local landmarks. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5142874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint Mary of La Seu d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt; in the town itself. Many trails also pass through charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4629023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calbinyà Village&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's cultural heritage and Romanesque architecture. The Segre Olympic Park also provides a unique setting for walks along the river.

Q: Are there more challenging hikes for experienced adventurers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, the proximity to the Alt Pirineu Natural Park and Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park means access to more demanding routes. These parks feature high mountains and largely untouched nature, offering trails with significant elevation gain and longer durations, often requiring good fitness and alpine experience. There are 2 difficult routes listed in the area, providing a challenge for seasoned adventurers.
tips:
XIII century cathedral. Located in the center of the city of La Seu de Urgell. We observe a beautiful stone façade and if we enter, which is highly recommended, we will be surprised by the interior cloister.

We can visit the museum.
On the outskirts of the town of Calbinyà, we find this religious building, currently in ruins. You can still see the bell tower.

Calbinyà is a beautiful town belonging to the municipality of Valles del Valira, near the Seu de Urgell. It is located on a hill that overlooks the Seu plain and where we can visit the Cal Serni museum. Also the church of Sant Tomàs de Calbinyà.

faqs.question: Is the La Seu de Urgell and Calbinya — PR-C 62 circular trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is classified as moderate and is generally suitable for all skill levels, including families. While it requires good fitness, the paths are easily accessible. Some sections may require sure-footedness, but it's a manageable challenge for those new to hiking.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the PR-C 62 circular trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the impressive Cathedral of Saint Mary of La Seu d'Urgell, the historic Ruins of Santa Margarida de Calbinyà, and the charming Calbinyà Village itself, known for its rural character and traditional agriculture.

faqs.question: What are the views like from the trail?
faqs.answer: From the village of Calbinyà, situated at an altitude of 1000 meters, you can enjoy extensive panoramic views. These vistas stretch from Andorra to Cerdanya, encompassing Pallars and the various valleys of Urgell, offering a truly picturesque experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for the PR-C 62 circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in La Seu d'Urgell. While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, La Seu d'Urgell offers various parking options within the town. Public transport access to La Seu d'Urgell is also available, making it convenient to reach the starting point.

faqs.question: Is the PR-C 62 circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Please ensure you carry waste bags and clean up after your pet to maintain the trail's cleanliness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The area around Calbinyà experiences a mild Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are typically ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike the PR-C 62 circular trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the PR-C 62 circular trail. It is a publicly accessible route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and surface condition of the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features easily accessible paths and is generally well-marked. While mostly straightforward, some sections may require sure-footedness. You can expect a varied landscape, combining natural paths with sections that might pass through or near rural areas.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the PR-C 62 trail is part of a wider network of well-marked paths in the region. It intersects with sections of other official trails such as E01, Ansenall, La Seu-Anserall, Estamariu - Calvinyà, La Rabasa, and the Camí de l'Últim Càtar, offering opportunities for longer explorations.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: La Seu d'Urgell, being the starting and ending point, offers a range of amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. In Calbinyà village, you might find rural accommodations like Cal Serni, which are known for their panoramic views and tranquil environment, perfect for a peaceful stop.
